deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor D1/H4/H1 swing highs/lows with age and untouched-state rules.
expected_failure_modes: Swing definitions can overfit; liquidity relevance depends on range structure and discretionary importance.
future_validation_method: Pre-register swing rules, then test with temporal split and OOS replay.
notes: Measurable only as a proxy; human liquidity importance is richer than a swing rule.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor M15/M5/M1 local swing points and recent range extremes.
expected_failure_modes: Short lookbacks create noisy micro-swings; same event can be counted repeatedly.
future_validation_method: Pre-register lookback and spacing; compare against matched controls.
notes: Useful for context, but not sufficient as a standalone Adelin feature.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Swing point inside a higher-timeframe range whose outer high/low remains untaken before anchor.
expected_failure_modes: Range boundaries are subjective; naive definitions can leak if future range completion is used.
future_validation_method: Freeze range construction rules before any outcome replay.
notes: Must use only already formed ranges, never future range boundaries.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Swing point outside the recent pre-anchor range using a fixed lookback window.
expected_failure_modes: External classification changes with lookback; shallow external levels can be low quality.
future_validation_method: Test only after fixed lookback and depth gates are registered.
notes: Should be a context feature, not an entry trigger by itself.

deterministic_definition_candidate: An H1 swing high/low is taken by pre-anchor candles and closes back inside or stalls beyond it.
expected_failure_modes: Close-back and stall definitions can be tuned; wick-only sweeps can be over-counted.
future_validation_method: Temporal split with source/session matched controls.
notes: Measurable now, but old simple sweep evidence is not sufficient for deployability.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Ordered pre-anchor M15 structure: approach, sweep, displacement or stall.
expected_failure_modes: Sequence grammar can become discretionary or tuned to outcomes.
future_validation_method: Use locked grammar and visual spot-checking before replay.
notes: Requires strict anti-leakage because post-entry reaction is tempting to include.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Three-candle imbalance with gap between candle 1 and candle 3 on a fixed timeframe.
expected_failure_modes: Mitigation rules and gap-size thresholds can be overfit.
possible_metric_formula: bullish: low_c3 > high_c1; bearish: high_c3 < low_c1; gap_size_pips >= threshold
future_validation_method: Pre-register timeframe, min gap, mitigation state, and replay split.
notes: Deterministic if timeframe and mitigation rules are locked.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Previously formed FVG that is mitigated pre-anchor and retested from the opposite side pre-anchor.
expected_failure_modes: Retest quality can become subjective; anchor-candle-only retests are leakage-prone.
future_validation_method: Use completed pre-anchor candles only; run OOS replay after fixed definition.
notes: Measurable, but stricter than generic FVG and easy to mislabel if anchor candle is used.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Rolling pre-anchor volume-at-price profile with HVN/LVN bins.
required_data: tick volume|volume-at-price bins or approximated candle volume
expected_failure_modes: Broker tick volume may not represent real volume; bin size and lookback change labels.
future_validation_method: Validate with fixed binning and broker-data sensitivity checks.
notes: Needs better volume data before it should carry much weight.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor candle with exceptional volume and body dominance breaking through a level.
expected_failure_modes: Volume source quality varies; large candles during news may distort interpretation.
future_validation_method: Register volume source and compare against news/session buckets.
notes: Potentially measurable, but only after volume data quality is audited.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Distance from swept or reaction level to round-number prices ending in 0.
expected_failure_modes: Can be over-weighted despite being only confluence; too many nearby levels reduce selectivity.
future_validation_method: Test only as pre-registered confluence, not as standalone signal.
notes: Measurable now, but must never be treated as sufficient by itself.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Static price grid where significant digits end in zero, measured by distance to candidate level.
expected_failure_modes: Threshold choice can inflate matches; grid alone has no edge evidence.
future_validation_method: Use source-matched controls around the same grid.
notes: Round levels are context only, not entry evidence.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor confluence zone from FVG, IFVG, volume crack, profile, or old rejection definitions.
expected_failure_modes: Zone boundaries and age rules can become discretionary; overlap logic can be tuned.
future_validation_method: Validate each zone subtype separately before using composite labels.
notes: The concept is central to Adelin, but only proxy-zone definitions are objectively measurable.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-entry wick/body and close-location behavior at a level before anchor.
expected_failure_modes: Humans judge rejection with context; wick ratios alone misread news and spread artifacts.
future_validation_method: Use locked candle morphology features plus manual review calibration.
notes: Potential feature, but previous rejection subset was not robust OOS.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor close back above/below swept level after taking liquidity.
expected_failure_modes: Waiting for reclaim can change the intended early-entry idea; late reclaim may be post-entry confirmation.
future_validation_method: Register whether reclaim is context-only or a separate late-entry hypothesis.
notes: Useful for risk tagging, but may conflict with Adelin's no-full-confirmation entry concept.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor narrow range around a level for a fixed number of candles before directional expansion.
expected_failure_modes: Expansion is often known only after anchor; pre-entry accumulation definitions can leak.
future_validation_method: Separate pre-anchor compression from post-anchor expansion outcome.
notes: Only the accumulation part is pre-entry; expansion must be measured later.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Post-entry movement speed after anchor, measured as an outcome rather than a pre-entry feature.
expected_failure_modes: Using this before entry would be direct leakage; it is valid only as replay outcome.
future_validation_method: Measure only in forward replay after pre-registered entry hypothesis.
notes: Outcome metric, not a valid pre-entry feature.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Large favorable movement after entry, treated only as replay outcome.
expected_failure_modes: Cannot be known pre-entry; long windows can overstate quality on XAUUSD.
future_validation_method: Measure with capped forward replay and matched controls.
notes: Never use as an entry filter.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor candle morphology: wick ratios, body ratio, close location, and range.
expected_failure_modes: Morphology ignores context and spread; thresholds can be tuned.
future_validation_method: Use fixed feature bins and OOS profile comparison.
notes: Measurable, but should be contextual metadata rather than standalone signal.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Large pre-anchor candle or sequence with body and range above rolling baseline.
expected_failure_modes: Large news candles can look like displacement; baseline choice matters.
future_validation_method: Temporal split by session and volatility regime.
notes: Measurable now as a candle feature.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor decline in range/ATR before a candidate level interaction.
expected_failure_modes: Expansion half of the phrase is post-anchor; compression thresholds are regime-sensitive.
future_validation_method: Use only pre-anchor compression; test outcomes separately.
notes: Useful as regime/context metadata, not proof of setup quality.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Anchor timestamp bucketed by UTC/local hour.
expected_failure_modes: DST/session calendar mistakes can misbucket events.
future_validation_method: Compare metrics by fixed hour buckets.
notes: Measurable now and safe as metadata.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Anchor timestamp mapped to Asia, London, New York, opens, or other.
expected_failure_modes: DST and broker-time conversion mistakes can create false session effects.
future_validation_method: Use session-matched controls.
notes: Measurable now and required for fair baseline comparison.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Distance and directional agreement among pre-anchor HTF and LTF levels.
expected_failure_modes: Alignment can be made too broad; level source mismatch can create false positives.
future_validation_method: Pre-register source hierarchy and distance thresholds.
notes: Composite alignment is promising only if all sub-definitions are locked first.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor close location relative to swept level, zone edge, and candle range.
expected_failure_modes: Close quality is often judged visually; late confirmation may conflict with early entry.
future_validation_method: Lock whether close quality is context-only or an entry gate.
notes: Measurable as proxy, but subjective as a discretionary phrase.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Continuation patterns after old Adelin signals are tracked only as risk/negative context.
required_data: OHLC candles|old signal exports if audited
expected_failure_modes: Old continuation behavior was toxic; positive use invites selection bias and strategy drift.
future_validation_method: If studied, test as a banned/blocked risk label, never as positive feature.
notes: BANNED_AS_POSITIVE_FEATURE; may only be reframed as risk or no-trade context.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor failed attempt to continue toward target after liquidity was taken.
expected_failure_modes: Failure is hard to know before the move has played out; target definition can leak.
future_validation_method: Use only completed pre-anchor failure structure and matched controls.
notes: Can be a reversal-risk context, not evidence for generic continuation.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor ATR/range percentile bucket using only completed candles.
expected_failure_modes: Small samples can collapse in one regime; ATR lookback must be fixed.
future_validation_method: Require no single regime bucket collapse in OOS validation.
notes: Measurable now and important for stratification.

deterministic_definition_candidate: Pre-anchor directional slope and range compression/expansion state.
expected_failure_modes: Trend/range definitions vary and can overfit; regime labels can lag.
future_validation_method: Freeze regime definition and report performance by regime bucket.
notes: Measurable as a proxy, but not a discretionary truth label.
